response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a full-stack web and systems developer whose career centers on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web infrastructure. The role's two non-negotiable pillars — substantial C++ experience on Windows and capital-markets/fixed-income domain knowledge — are both effectively absent. Her only documented C++ work is a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor-thesis audio project; C++ does not even appear in her listed skills. She has no exposure to SQL Server or Oracle, no Windows platform development, and no meaningful capital-markets or fixed-income background. CI/CD familiarity and a Java background are genuine positives but carry little weight given the depth of the gaps.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, containerized deployments) aligns with a desirable optional skill
Java usage across multiple roles satisfies one of the optional desirable skills listed in the JD
Strong communication track record evidenced by conference talks, full-day workshops, and open-source community engagement
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — roughly six months in 2014–2015 with no follow-on, and the language is absent from her own skills list
No fixed-income, derivatives, investment-banking, or broader capital-markets domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
SQL experience is limited to MySQL/MongoDB in web contexts; no SQL Server or Oracle exposure, and no evidence of complex financial-data querying
